AMCO United Holding ( (HK:0630) ) just unveiled an announcement.

AMCO United Holding has called its annual general meeting for 26 June 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will consider the adoption of the 2025 audited consolidated financial statements and re-election of three directors, including one executive and two independent non-executive directors. Investors will also vote on the re-appointment of CCTH CPA Limited as auditor and a general mandate allowing the board to issue up to 20% of the company’s existing share capital, a move that preserves financial flexibility for potential fundraising and corporate actions while subjecting future dilution to defined limits and shareholder oversight.

The latest announcement is out from Sincere Watch (Hong Kong) ( (HK:0444) ).

Sincere Watch (Hong Kong) Limited has confirmed the composition of its board, led by Chairman and Chief Executive Officer Mr. Zhang Xiaoliang, along with two other executive directors, one non-executive director and two independent non-executive directors. The company has also detailed the membership of its four key board committees—Audit, Remuneration, Nomination and Investment—clarifying leadership roles and responsibilities in a move that reinforces its corporate governance framework and oversight structure for stakeholders.

The Audit Committee is composed of independent non-executive directors Mr. Yu Zhenxin and Mr. Zong Hao, underscoring independent financial oversight. The Remuneration, Nomination and Investment Committees are each chaired by a mix of independent and executive directors, which may help balance management influence and independent judgment in decisions on pay, board appointments and capital deployment.

data.title: Sincere Watch (Hong Kong) Faces Board Gaps After Director Resignations
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Sincere Watch (Hong Kong) ( (HK:0444) ).

Sincere Watch (Hong Kong) Limited announced the resignation of Executive Director Hong Sze Lung, who is leaving to devote more time to his family, and Independent Non-executive Director and Audit Committee chairman Chow Chi Fai, who is stepping down to focus on other business ventures. Both departing directors stated they have no disagreements with the board and no matters that need to be brought to shareholders’ attention.

Following Chow’s resignation, the company no longer meets several Hong Kong listing requirements regarding the minimum number and proportion of independent non-executive directors and the size and qualifications within its audit committee. The board said it will make its best efforts to recruit suitable candidates and restore full compliance with the Listing Rules within three months from 30 April 2026, and will issue further announcements when appropriate.

More about Sincere Watch (Hong Kong)

Sincere Watch (Hong Kong) Limited is a Hong Kong-listed company in the watch and luxury timepiece sector. The company operates under stock code 444 on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange and is governed by a board comprising executive, non-executive, and independent non-executive directors, in line with local listing regulations.

The latest update is out from Freetech Road Recycling Technology (Holdings) Ltd. ( (HK:6888) ).

Freetech Road Recycling Technology (Holdings) Ltd. plans to overhaul its constitutional documents by replacing its existing articles of association with amended and restated articles aligned with Hong Kong’s updated listing framework. The move reflects the company’s efforts to keep pace with regulatory and market practice changes, including support for hybrid corporate meetings and broader use of electronic communication.

The proposed amendments aim to bring the company’s governance structure in line with the expanded paperless listing regime, electronic dissemination of corporate communications, hybrid and electronic shareholder meetings, the new treasury shares regime and an uncertificated securities market initiative. These changes, which remain subject to shareholder approval via special resolution at the annual general meeting scheduled for 29 May 2026, signal a shift toward more flexible, technology-enabled shareholder engagement and modernized capital management options.

More about Freetech Road Recycling Technology (Holdings) Ltd.

Freetech Road Recycling Technology (Holdings) Ltd. is a Hong Kong-listed company focused on road recycling technologies and related services for the infrastructure and transportation sectors. The group develops and applies pavement recycling solutions that support more efficient, cost-effective and sustainable road maintenance, positioning it within the broader construction and infrastructure technology industry.

An announcement from Yues International Holdings Group Limited ( (HK:1529) ) is now available.

Yues International Holdings Group Limited has announced a delay in completing its acquisition of 100% equity interest in Hangzhou Yihang Logistics Co., Ltd., a logistics firm in mainland China. The deal remains a key step in the group’s efforts to broaden its logistics footprint and strengthen its position in the Chinese market.

The company said that while the preliminary review by government authorities has been passed, more time is needed to finalize changes in industrial and commercial registration for the equity transfer. As a result, the purchaser and vendor have mutually agreed to extend the completion deadline from 30 April 2026 to 31 May 2026, with all other terms of the transaction unchanged.

BOCOM International Holdings Co. Ltd. ( (HK:3329) ) just unveiled an announcement.

BOCOM International’s wholly owned subsidiary Preferred Investment has acquired an additional US$7.61 million in principal amount of Macquarie Group notes on the over-the-counter market for about US$6.93 million, funded with internal resources. Coupled with a March purchase of US$4 million in Macquarie Bank notes, the aggregated transactions now qualify as discloseable under Hong Kong listing rules, underscoring the group’s ongoing deployment of capital into fixed-income instruments issued by major global financial institutions.

The latest note purchase, involving securities listed in Frankfurt and sourced from Nomura International, follows earlier acquisitions via Jefferies International and Australia and New Zealand Banking Group. By channeling its own funds into these Macquarie-linked notes while maintaining arm’s-length dealings with independent counterparties, BOCOM International is expanding its investment portfolio and potentially enhancing yield, which may modestly influence its risk profile and return mix within the competitive regional investment banking and brokerage landscape.

data.title: Sichuan Expressway Unit Ends RMB100 Million Sand and Gravel Supply Contract
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Sichuan Expressway Co ( (HK:0107) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Sichuan Expressway Company Limited has terminated a sand and gravel procurement contract previously signed between its indirect non-wholly owned subsidiary Sichuan Chengyu Xingshu Supply Chain Management Company Limited and the LM Project Management Department of the Tianmei-Le Expressway Project of Sichuan Transportation Construction Lingzhou Construction Company Limited. The move follows business adjustments at the supply chain subsidiary that removed the basis for continuing the arrangement, under which supplies of manufactured sand and crushed stone had been expected to amount to no more than RMB100 million.

Under a termination agreement signed on 30 April 2026, both parties will complete settlement of payments for goods, performance guarantees and other amounts incurred during the contract period, with the purchaser required to pay remaining goods payments within three months after settlement procedures are finalized. The company stated that the termination was conducted on normal commercial terms, is fair and reasonable, and is not expected to have a material impact on its overall operations or financial position, suggesting limited implications for shareholders and project stakeholders.

More about Sichuan Expressway Co

Sichuan Expressway Company Limited is a PRC-incorporated joint stock company primarily engaged in the investment, construction, operation and related services of expressways and transportation infrastructure in Sichuan Province. Through subsidiaries such as Sichuan Chengyu Xingshu Supply Chain Management Company Limited, it also participates in ancillary logistics and supply chain businesses supporting regional transport projects.

data.title: Radiance Holdings’ Key Subsidiary Swings to Q1 Loss on Lower Revenue
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Radiance Holdings (Group) Company Limited ( (HK:9993) ).

Radiance Holdings has disclosed a summary of the unaudited first-quarter 2026 financial results of its indirect non-wholly owned subsidiary, Radiance Group Co., Ltd., as required by PRC rules tied to the unit’s outstanding debt instruments. The property-focused subsidiary reported total assets of RMB76.681 billion and total liabilities of RMB60.398 billion as of 31 March 2026, with equity attributable to owners slightly down to RMB16.283 billion and cash and cash equivalents essentially stable at RMB827 million.

For the three months ended 31 March 2026, Radiance Group’s operating revenue fell to RMB1.643 billion from RMB2.417 billion a year earlier, while gross profit margin narrowed to 13.50%, resulting in an operating loss of RMB23 million and a net loss of RMB19 million, compared with a profit in the prior-year period. The company highlighted that these unaudited figures, prepared under PRC accounting standards, are not directly comparable with Radiance Holdings’ Hong Kong reporting and do not provide a full picture of the group’s consolidated operating or financial condition, a caveat relevant for investors assessing credit and equity risk tied to the subsidiary’s debt.

More about Radiance Holdings (Group) Company Limited

Radiance Holdings (Group) Company Limited is a Cayman Islands–incorporated investment holding company listed in Hong Kong that operates through subsidiaries in China’s property and related sectors. Its indirect non-wholly owned unit Radiance Group Co., Ltd. issues onshore debt financing instruments in the PRC and reports quarterly financials under China Accounting Standards for Business Enterprises.

data.title: China Power’s SPIC Hydropower Q1 Net Profit Jumps Despite Revenue Decline
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from China Power International Development ( (HK:2380) ) is now available.

China Power International Development reported that its 55.13%-owned subsidiary SPIC Hydropower posted an 18.55% year-on-year decline in first-quarter 2026 revenue to RMB2.47 billion, but net profit attributable to shareholders surged 168% to RMB393 million. Basic earnings per share tripled to RMB0.09, supported by higher underlying profitability, as net profit excluding extraordinary items rose 176.2%, while total assets and shareholder equity grew modestly, though the company cautioned that these figures reflect only the subsidiary and not the group’s overall financial position.

SPIC Hydropower’s comparative figures for early 2025 were restated under PRC GAAP rules for business combinations under common control following an asset restructuring that made it a subsidiary of China Power. The restructuring has allowed SPIC Hydropower to account for acquired operations as if owned in prior periods, clarifying performance trends for investors, but China Power advised shareholders and potential investors not to rely solely on the subsidiary’s results when assessing the group as a whole.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2380) stock is a Hold with a HK$3.30 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on China Power International Development stock, see the HK:2380 Stock Forecast page.

More about China Power International Development

China Power International Development is a Hong Kong–incorporated power producer with a growing focus on hydropower through its majority-owned subsidiary SPIC Hydropower Co., Ltd., which is listed on the Shanghai Stock Exchange. The group operates within China’s electricity sector, expanding its renewable generation portfolio and consolidating assets under common control to strengthen its position in low‑carbon power.

Fujing Holdings Co., Limited has called its annual general meeting for 22 May 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will review and approve the audited consolidated financial statements, directors’ report and auditor’s report for the financial year ended 31 December 2025. The meeting will also consider the re-election of two executive directors and one independent non-executive director, with authority for the board to set their remuneration, and the re-appointment of McMillan Woods (Hong Kong) CPA Limited as auditor.

Shareholders will further vote on granting the board a general mandate to issue up to 20% of the company’s issued share capital, including the potential sale or transfer of treasury shares and the issuance of convertible instruments, subject to certain exclusions such as rights issues and employee share schemes. This proposed mandate would enhance the company’s financial and capital-raising flexibility, potentially affecting future equity dilution and signaling management’s intention to maintain room for strategic financing or corporate actions within Hong Kong listing rules.

More about Fujing Holdings Company., Limited

Fujing Holdings Co., Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange under stock code 2497. The group operates through its subsidiaries and is subject to Hong Kong listing rules and corporate governance requirements, with its board comprising executive and independent non-executive directors and an external auditor based in Hong Kong.

Sunshine Oilsands ( (HK:2012) ) has shared an update.

Sunshine Oilsands Ltd. has announced a further delay in sending a shareholder circular related to a proposed subscription of convertible bonds under a specific mandate, which is tied to debt settlement and requires independent shareholder approval. The circular, originally expected by April 30, 2026, will now be dispatched on or before May 29, 2026, as the company needs additional time to finalize and incorporate required information, and it cautioned investors that completion of the transaction remains subject to conditions and may not proceed.

More about Sunshine Oilsands

Sunshine Oilsands Ltd. is a Calgary-based public oil and gas company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, focused on developing its extensive oil sands and petroleum and natural gas leases in Canada’s Athabasca region. The company holds interests in approximately one million acres and is currently prioritizing milestone development at its West Ells project, which has an initial production target of 5,000 barrels of oil per day.
